title: Level 993.1
wikitext:
{{Class|top-color=b|p1-color=b|p2-color=b|p3-color=b|p1=3|p2=2}} == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Cloud9.jpg|caption=Depiction of how Level 993.1 looks as one first enters.}} '''Level 993.1''', commonly known and referred to as Cloud Nine, will appear as an average house upon entry of the level, though one will come to realize that this structure is suspended in the sky by unknown means. This level has many strange properties and very noticeable similarities to [[Level 993]], as every area in the level heavily represents the sky in one way or another. Upon further investigation of this level, researchers say that the level is likely infinite, however there are still multiple parts of the level that remain unexplored to this day, making this undetermined. Going further into the first section of the level will inevitably cause one to find stranger areas, often shifting into random or unrecognizable structures that heavily differ from the initial area encountered upon entry, and some that do not even exist in the Frontrooms. Going any further will reveal the properties of the level undergoing drastic changes. One of the most strange and dangerous properties of the level is the highly unstable geometry which enables rooms and areas to switch and take place somewhere else. Rather than simply teleporting, two rooms of the same shape will "detach themselves" and float to the place of their counterpart, taking place in the location their counterpart switched from. This can potentially be fatal as the process will not stop if someone or something gets in the way, meaning that if a person or thing happens to be blocking the path the structure has to follow to replace the corresponding structure, it will simply keep going and crush anything attempting to block it. {{Blurimage|file=C L O U D S.jpg|caption=Level 993.1 as one goes further in.}} Supplies are somewhat common in Level 993.1, though they are often found in random and unpredictable locations, commonly knocked over on the floor. These supplies often manifest as food and drinks from Level 993 such as Cloud Milk, although there have also been reports of miscellaneous Skyspace food and drink concepts, which were rejected according to an investigation in Level 993. These items can vary, though the most common ones often have generic brand names such as "Sunny Day Chips". Rejected items from Level 993 will become extremely common in Level 993.1 as one goes on, therefore some speculate that the items may have been sent to this mysterious sublevel to rot and somehow managed to multiply by unknown means (see Concept Theory). Oftentimes the Cloud Milk or other liquids found in this level are spilt, implying that they may have randomly noclipped into this level from Level 993. Other common supplies such as Almond Water are extremely rare; fortunately, there have been no reports of Liquid Pain or any other life threatening or harmful objects. {{Blurimage|file=Do you want to wake up.jpg|caption=How Level 993.1 changes as one goes far enough.}} == Theories == === Concept Theory=== Some speculate that the level is entirely the result of rejected Skyspace concepts that managed to shift into this level. The theory is supported by the trash bins found in the offices of Level 993 which some have reported mysteriously leading to this level. Although, there have also been unexplained reports of unfinished item concepts that have not even been created or attempted yet appearing in the level, which may imply this level is also constructed of unfinished ideas or ideas that are a work in progress. To this day, the Concept Theory is the most popular one. ===Out of Bounds Theory=== Others believe that this level exists beyond the sky of Level 993. One claims to have managed to jump out of one of Level 993's windows and onto a strange cloud that was somehow physically able to carry them to Level 993.1 where they landed in the house. While this theory may be supported by the first photo taken of the level, it is undetermined who took the photo and how the cloud got there. This theory is very controversial and rarely brought up among wanderers. ==Entities== {{Blurimage|file=Up in the clouds.webp|caption=How Level 993.1 appears when one wanders too far. Exits will become much more rare and the level will no longer change upon reaching this stage.}} No entities have been spotted on this level, however there is proof of supposed entities that may have formerly existed on the level. Proof of this includes small bite marks on some cloud milk cartons and bags of Sunny Day Chips being chewed open and half the contents of the bag being gone. The size of the bite marks are remarkably small, which may imply the entities that used to inhabit the level are non-hostile. Unfortunately, no other traces of any entity type has been found in this level. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the strange and unpredictable geometry of the level, no attempts of making Colonies or Outposts has yet been attempted. ==Environmental Dangers == While it has been said before that two rooms may detach and switch places, which is considered the most dangerous aspect of the level due to the varying speed of which it happens, there are multiple other hazards one may want to avoid such as shifting hallways, occasional wet floors, and falling ceiling tiles. There are also no railings in the outside areas of this level, or the areas that expose one to the sky, therefore falling off the level is potentially fatal as there is no confirmation of what lies below, though many believe it will still lead to one falling to their death in the Frontrooms. ==Discovery == The first reported sighting of this level was during an individual exploration of Level 993. The Wanderer in question claims to have come across a door which had a cloud engraved into it. They said to have found this level upon opening the door. After sharing their findings, they lived there for about 2 weeks before tragically dying after being decapitated while two structures were undergoing the process of switching places. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * As mentioned earlier, one can enter this level by opening a door with a cloud engraved into it on [[Level 993]] or other levels that are Class 3 or below according to other witnesses. * Noclip into a trash can in Level 993 === Exits === * Noclipping into a cloud will take one back to [[Level 993]], however this is extremely risky as the clouds must be in the sky. * If one heads far enough in this level and noclips, it is possible and very likely to enter [[The Broken]]. * Noclipping is feasible, however it is difficult and leads to a completely random level. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:TheAgentAnonymous|TheAgentAnonymous]]
}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 993.1: "Cloud Nine"}} {{Background|file=Isnt right.jpg}} {{Audio|file=Cloudnine.mp3|volume=1}} __NOTOC__ [[Category:Class 2]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Sky]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Levels]] [[zh:Level 993.1]]
